_comment: Machine-readable lock for ELEVM's external bootstrap inputs. Consumed by scripts/env_doctor.py and (from Step 2 onward) the bootstrap tooling. Documented in scripts/vectors/SOURCES.md. Do not duplicate these literals elsewhere; update this file and let consumers read it.

execution_specs._comment: Legacy BlockchainTests fixtures live under <root>/tests/fixtures/ethereum_tests, an independent nested checkout (see ethereum_tests below), not an execution-specs submodule.

ethereum_tests._comment: Ignored by execution-specs' .gitignore; not tracked as a Git submodule of execution-specs.

eest._comment_env_var: EEST_ROOT names the top-level install directory containing the archive and its extracted fixtures/ tree. It is distinct from scripts/check.sh's ELEVM_FIXTURES, which instead points directly at a tier's leaf fixture directory (and already defaults to <EEST_ROOT>/fixtures/blockchain_tests for --bls). The two variables are not interchangeable.
eest._comment_layout: Every member of the archive is a regular file whose path begins with the fixtures_subpath prefix, so extracting into <EEST_ROOT> yields <EEST_ROOT>/fixtures/. The archive contains no directory, symlink, hardlink, or special members; the safe extractor and deep verifier reject anything else.
eest._comment_metadata: Release-identity fields recorded inside the archive's own .meta/fixtures.ini ([fixtures] section). Fast-mode verification confirms the extracted tree carries this exact provenance; the values are pinned here rather than derived from release_tag because the upstream git ref (tests-v5.4.0) differs from the release download tag (v5.4.0).

python_oracle._comment: Python 3.11.9 is an exact requirement, not merely a 3.11-series preference: the oracle includes native extension wheels and its generated bytes are accepted only from the tested interpreter/package closure. The complete 18-package runtime closure is hash-locked in requirements_lock. execution-specs itself is intentionally absent from that lock; generators import from the separately manifest-pinned checkout, avoiding a machine-local path entry.
